_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an index averaging prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice with 2015 as the base year (100), currently stands at 141, marking a 41% increase since 2015. This rise reflects a recent and uneven climb in global soft commodity prices. The index aggregates diverse commodities, masking significant individual variations. Price gains have not been uniform across all components.
notes.movers: Coffee prices have surged the most, nearly doubling with a 99% increase since 2015, followed by orange juice at a 34% rise. Wheat and sugar have seen more modest changes of 19% and 13%, respectively. These price developments largely stem from weather-related disturbances and crop diseases—such as frosts and droughts in coffee-growing regions and citrus greening plus hurricanes impacting orange juice production—rather than broad-based inflation. The soft commodity markets involved are relatively thin, meaning price swings can be large due to these supply shocks.
notes.why: The headline increase in the breakfast basket is driven by a handful of weather-sensitive crops with exposed production risks. Given the nature of agricultural cycles and susceptibility to environmental factors, elevated input costs can persist over multiple years. This underscores the importance of considering supply-side vulnerability when assessing price trends in soft commodities.
